title: The Long Road
content:
Long the road,
    Till Love came down it!
    Dark the life,
    Till Love did crown it!
    Dark the life,
    And long the road,
    Till Love came
    To share the load!
    For the touch
    Of Love transfigures
    All the road
    And all its rigours.
    Life and Death,
    Love's touch transfigures.
    Life and Death
    And all that lies
    In between,
    Love sanctifies.
    Once the heavenly spark is lighted,
    Once in love two hearts united,
    Nevermore
    Shall aught that was be
    As before.
meaning:
William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Long Road"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
